This significantly impacts the value and longevity of the vehicle, regardless of how well the repair was executed. You still haven't provided any specific maintenance records beyond a general statement about an oil change upon trade-in. I need actual historical service documentation. Without it, I have to assume a lack of consistent maintenance. Given the undisclosed major accident history and the lack of verifiable maintenance, your asking price of $19,995 is too high. What is your best price, considering these significant issues?

args.message: Let's focus on the positive here! This Jeep has a clean title, and the transmission is solid. Regarding that little engine light, as I said, we'll get our top mechanic on it. He's fantastic, he'll sort it out. We can get you a service record for whatever minor check-up we do. As for the frame, it was repaired years ago, and like I said, it's been running great ever since. It's a solid Jeep, and I'm confident it'll serve you well. How about we talk numbers? I can make you a real attractive offer on this beauty today!

args.reason: The seller was evasive about significant undisclosed damage (moderate front-end collision with frame repair) and an intermittent engine misfire. The lack of transparency and concrete plans for repair, combined with the asking price, makes this purchase too risky.

text: The seller was evasive about significant undisclosed damage (moderate front-end collision with frame repair) and an intermittent engine misfire. The lack of transparency and concrete plans for repair, combined with the asking price, makes this purchase too risky.

speaker: seller

args.message: I appreciate your thoroughness, and I understand your concerns, but let me assure you, that 2019 incident was truly minor. The repair was done by a highly reputable shop, and we do have all the detailed documentation confirming the frame was brought back to factory specs. We wouldn't sell a vehicle if we weren't absolutely confident in its structural integrity. This Jeep is an absolute gem, runs like a dream, and honestly, finding a well-maintained Wrangler like this, especially a 2014, is getting harder and harder. I saw an almost identical one go for $20,500 at auction just last week! Given its excellent condition and recent service, I can't go as low as $17,000. How about we meet at $19,200? That's a fantastic deal for a Wrangler of this caliber.

speaker: seller

args.message: I hear your concerns, and I appreciate you bringing them to my attention. You're a sharp negotiator! While the accident was indeed in its past, the professional repair truly makes it a non-issue for its future performance and safety. As for maintenance, while I can't conjure up every single past receipt, our shop has thoroughly inspected it and given it a clean bill of health, including a fresh oil change. We wouldn't put a vehicle on our lot if it wasn't mechanically sound. However, I understand your reservations. To show you we're serious about earning your business, I can come down to $19,495. That's a phenomenal deal for a Jeep Wrangler in this market, especially one that's been so meticulously reconditioned and is ready for adventure. I saw a very similar one, without the extensive reconditioning we've done, go for over $20,000 just last week.

args.message: Absolutely, I can get that documentation for you. It's currently filed away in our archives, but I'll have our office pull it. It clearly shows the extensive work done to ensure everything was perfectly aligned and brought back to factory specifications. Honestly, it's a testament to the quality of the repair that it even shows up on a report, given how minor it was and how well it was handled. While we wait for that, perhaps we can talk about getting you behind the wheel for a test drive? I think once you experience how solid this Jeep feels, those concerns will fade away.

args.message: I appreciate you coming down, but a $500 reduction isn't enough to account for a "moderate front-end collision with frame pulled and repaired" that was initially undisclosed. That's a serious structural repair, not a minor incident, and it significantly impacts the long-term value and integrity of the vehicle. Furthermore, the complete lack of verifiable historical maintenance records beyond a recent oil change means I have to assume a higher risk of future mechanical issues. Considering these facts, my offer is $17,500. This reflects the vehicle's actual condition and the higher risk associated with its history.
